prompt_rules: world-map forest mass tiles, not individual tree sprites
include forest center, four edges, outer corners, inner corners, road cut-through, darker interior, and shadowed edge variants
the forest should read as a geographic region with an organic outline

prompt:
Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, soft warm GOLDEN-HOUR sunlight, hand-painted Ghibli-like look. A top-down sacred GROVE CANOPY (鎮守の森) autotile using the world_forest_mass_16 preset — the prime goal is to eliminate the 'square forest' problem by building in soft, graduated, non-rectangular edges. CANOPY STYLE: rounded broadleaf tree crowns (camphor, evergreen oak, chinquapin) in deep rich emerald and jade green, dappled sunlit highlights on the topmost leaf pads, deep cool blue-green shadow pooling beneath interior canopies. Individual tree crowns are large and rounded with painterly leaf-cluster highlights, NOT a flat solid green mass. Edge crowns are LOWER, SMALLER, and LIGHTER than interior crowns, creating a natural silhouette variation. STRICT 4x4 TILESET LAYOUT matching world_forest_mass_16 convention: Row1 CENTER (4 cells) = full-density interior canopy variations — deep saturated greens, closely packed rounded crowns, dappled light; each cell shifts slightly in crown arrangement and dapple position to prevent a tiled pattern. Row2 DIRECTIONAL EDGES (4 cells) = N, S, W, E: the named side shows the forest-to-grass boundary; the HIGH-LOW-DIFFERENCE 3-POINT SET is embedded per edge frame: (1) the outermost ring of smaller, brighter, lower understory shrubs and leafy fringe (明るい縁, 16-24 px wide), (2) the tall dark vertical trunks and deep shadow behind the shrub ring (縦面, 40-60 px of dark canopy depth), (3) a soft cast shadow on the open grass outside the forest (下影, 12-20 px dark fade). The boundary between forest and grass must be a NON-STRAIGHT, organically wavy/scalloped line — individual crown bulges protrude unevenly into the grass zone. Row3 OUTER CORNERS (4 cells) = NW, NE, SW, SE: organically rounded outer corners where the forest edge curves — a gentle scalloped arc of fringe shrubs and partial crown bulges; no 90-degree hard corner is permitted; the corner must look like a natural forest promontory. Row4 ACCENT/TRANSITION (4 cells) = cell12: thin ONE-CELL forest strip (small isolated tree or bush hedge, forest thinner than a single interior cell), cell13: forest-edge with low ground-level bush transition (fern, moss, low-light plants), cell14: a forest shadow edge accent (just the cast shadow and a few dark trunks, for use as the outermost shadow overlay on open grass), cell15: a forest inner-corner concave tile (open clearing biting into the forest — interior forest on three sides, small gap on one). All 16 cells filled edge-to-edge, FULLY OPAQUE, seamlessly tileable. No isometric perspective, no text, no character, no UI.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
